AdaptationContribution.description:
Per-session adaptation signal by capacity axis.

Computed by the dose engine from aggregated phi_adapt vectors and session
load. Consumed by state_update to drive explicit capacity gains.
AdaptationContribution.title: AdaptationContribution

CapacityConfidence.description:
Per-axis model uncertainty about ``CapacityState``, as a variance proxy.

Higher variance ⇒ the model is less sure ⇒ a benchmark corrects that axis more
(scalar Kalman gain). The seed prior is high-variance — a weak prior that yields
to data — benchmarks shrink it and time grows it. Tracked for capacity axes only
(fatigue/tissue are transient, re-driven each session). See ADR-0036; the scalar
here generalizes to the EKF's covariance later (ADR-0015).
CapacityConfidence.title: CapacityConfidence

CapacityState.description: X_t — capacity / adaptation ceiling components (higher = better).
CapacityState.title: CapacityState

DashboardBundleOut.description: Latest primary-anchor observations plus derived KPI snapshots.
DashboardBundleOut.title: DashboardBundleOut

ExerciseEntry.description:
A single exercise within a session log.

`phi_*` and `energy_mix` are resolved from the Exercise DB row by the
service layer before the dose engine runs. They are left blank in
client-submitted logs; the engine falls back to modality defaults when absent.
ExerciseEntry.title: ExerciseEntry

ExercisePrescription.description: A single prescribed exercise within a session.
ExercisePrescription.title: ExercisePrescription

FatigueState.description: F_t — multi-component fatigue (0–100). Higher = more fatigued.
FatigueState.title: FatigueState

next_step.default: Call GET /v1/next-session?goal=Strength to get first prescription
next_step.title: Next Step

PrescriptionExplanation.description: Why this session — state drivers, constraints, sources.

prescription_branch.description: Internal prescriber branch id (safety, readiness, goal path)
prescription_branch.title: Prescription Branch

score.description: Template-aligned fit score vs twin state (0–1)
score.title: Score

structured_template_name.description: Display name for structured coaching template (v2)
structured_template_name.title: Structured Template Name

PrescriptionExplanation.title: PrescriptionExplanation

ProfileUpdate.description:
Partial update — only fields present in the request body are written.

Nullable fields (lifts, biometrics) accept an explicit ``null`` to clear a
previously stored value; omitting a field leaves it untouched.
ProfileUpdate.title: ProfileUpdate

ReadinessComponent.description: How a single acute-wellness signal nudged readiness.

baseline.description: Personal rolling baseline, or the default anchor
baseline.title: Baseline

contribution.description: Direction-signed, clamped deviation in [-1, 1] (1 = a full unit better than baseline)
contribution.title: Contribution

ReadinessComponent.title: ReadinessComponent

kpi_flags.description: Soft signals from KPIs (e.g. elevated run fatigue factor)
kpi_flags.title: Kpi Flags

ReadinessScore.description:
The one backend-owned readiness number (PDR-0005) + its breakdown.

``readiness`` is ``None`` only when there is no modeled ``AthleteState`` to
anchor against — wellness modulates the model, it is not a standalone score.

as_of.description: Timestamp of the modeled state used
as_of.title: As Of

wellness_delta.description: Signed acute-wellness adjustment applied, in 0–100 points (0 if no sample)
wellness_delta.title: Wellness Delta

ReadinessScore.title: ReadinessScore

StressDose.description:
Stress dose: six-dimensional engine vector plus legacy scalars for clients.

`adaptation_contribution` is the session's positive adaptation signal per
capacity axis. Used by state_update for explicit capacity gains.
StressDose.title: StressDose

StressDoseSix.description: D_t — session stress dose in six dimensions (non-negative).
StressDoseSix.title: StressDoseSix

UnifiedStateVector.description:
Digital twin snapshot S(t): legacy scalars + decomposed engine vectors.

Legacy fields remain for backward-compatible API clients; they are derived
from (capacity_x, fatigue_f, tissue_t) on each update.
UnifiedStateVector.title: UnifiedStateVector

WellnessSampleIn.description: One acute daily-wellness reading to ingest (manual entry or provider pull).
WellnessSampleIn.title: WellnessSampleIn

benchmark_results.description: Optional benchmark key/value payload for benchmark sessions.
benchmark_results.title: Benchmark Results

dominant_movement_pattern.description: e.g. squat | hinge | run — defaults inferred from modality
dominant_movement_pattern.title: Dominant Movement Pattern

estimated_sets.description: If set, refines volume term in dose law
estimated_sets.title: Estimated Sets

exercises.description: Per-exercise breakdown. When present, dose reflects actual exercise phi vectors.
exercises.title: Exercises

life_stress_inverse.description: 1 = Very high life stress, 10 = No life stress
life_stress_inverse.title: Life Stress Inverse

novelty.description: >1 = novel / high coordination demand for this athlete
novelty.title: Novelty

planned_session_id.description: If provided, marks this log as fulfillment of the planned session.
planned_session_id.title: Planned Session Id

WorkoutPrescription.description: Next-session recommendation. Legacy fields required; `why` optional for old clients.
WorkoutPrescription.title: WorkoutPrescription

schema.title: Response Get Run Program Program Run Get
Response Get Strength Program Program Strength Get
Response Get Benchmark Definitions V1 Benchmarks Definitions Get
Response Get Benchmark Observations V1 Benchmarks Observations Get

get.description: DEV-friendly version that auto-initializes baseline state.

parameters.description: Training goal to prescribe for; defaults to the athlete's primary goal.
parameters.name: goal

schema.description: Training goal to prescribe for; defaults to the athlete's primary goal.
schema.title: Goal
Response List Blocks V1 Planning Blocks Get
Response List Sessions V1 Planning Sessions Get

get.description:
Return all weak-point rows for the current user.

By default only active (unresolved) rows are returned.
Pass active_only=false to include resolved rows.

schema.title: Response List Weak Points V1 Weak Points  Get

delete.description:
Hard-delete a weak-point row owned by the current user.

Returns 204 No Content on success, 404 if not found or wrong user.

patch.description:
Update confidence, note, and/or resolved_at on a weak-point row.

Only fields explicitly present in the request body are applied.
Sending resolved_at=null re-opens a resolved weak point.

schema.title: Response List Wellness V1 Wellness Get
